Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-31 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two starkly different momentum profiles at the top of the rankings. CLMT (#1, score 94.3) displays textbook strong momentum with consistent gains across all timeframes (2.37% daily through 172.57% annual), passes 4 of 5 ratio consistency checks, and notably maintains a 0.99 resilience score on bad SPY days—meaning it actually gained when the market fell. In sharp contrast, FSLY (#2) shows extreme volatility with a massive 13.85% single-day jump but a concerning 0.22 ratio (1-week return collapsing to just 3%) and a negative 0.19 bad_spy_score, indicating it tends to decline when SPY underperforms. CLMT's steady 94.1% six-month return paired with its outperformance versus SPY (23.05%), QQQ (23.78%), and even gold IAU (16.13%) over one week suggests cleaner momentum mechanics, while FSLY's explosive 346% annual gain is shadowed by its whipsaw trading pattern and weak market-stress performance. The dashboard appears to be highlighting quality momentum (CLMT) versus quantity of returns (FSLY), with only CLMT's momentum signal flagged as "overextended"
